opinion.h: CARR v. NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE APPELLATE DIVISION OF THE SUPREME COURT OF NEW YORK, THIRD JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT. No. 265. Decided October 8, 1962.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Louis J. Lefkowitz, Attorney General of New York, and Paxton Blair, Solicitor General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE GOLDBERG took no part in the consideration or decision of this case.
